commit.message:
fix: use RouterLink directly in BtBrandingLogo — avoid BVN BNavbarBrand to='' bug

BVN 0.43 BLink resolves 'to' via resolveDynamicComponent('RouterLink') using
the app instance's router. If the component tree has any Vue Router dedup
issue, 'to' arrives as empty string and router.push('') is a no-op.

Using RouterLink directly is more reliable and semantically correct.

commit.message:
fix(i18n): correct mergeLocaleMessage strategy for vue-i18n v9 legacy:false

In vue-i18n v9 with legacy:false, app.config.globalProperties.$i18n is a
stripped proxy object exposing only locale/t/d/n/etc — it has no .global
and no .mergeLocaleMessage.

Fix: accept the host's i18n instance via options.i18n so we can call
options.i18n.global.mergeLocaleMessage() directly. Falls back to legacy
mode ($i18n.global) and fresh install as before.

Host apps must pass { i18n } to app.use(CommunityEngineVue, { i18n }).

commit.message:
feat(session): auto-restore AI sidebar on Neovim session load (#25)

When a Neovim session is restored (e.g. via `nvim -S Session.vim` or
`:source Session.vim`), aiwaku now automatically reconnects to the
last-used AI tmux session without any user interaction.

The mechanism relies on Neovim's built-in session saving: `mksession`
preserves terminal buffer names. aiwaku names its terminal buffers
`aiwaku://ai-<session-name>`, so on `SessionLoadPost` the plugin can
recover the session name from the saved buffer, verify the tmux session
still exists, and reconnect the sidebar.

- If the sidebar was visible when the session was saved, the same
  window is reused and a live terminal is opened. Focus stays in the
  user's editing window (or terminal insert mode is preserved if the
  sidebar itself was focused).
- If the sidebar was hidden, only `state.current_session` is restored
  so the next `toggle()` reconnects naturally without opening a new
  split.
- If the tmux session no longer exists, a warning is shown and the
  orphaned ghost buffer is cleaned up.

Multiple Neovim instances are safe: each instance restores from its
own session file, which contains its own buffer names, so instances
never pick up each other's sessions.

A new `restore_on_session_load` config option (default `true`) lets
users opt out.

- open_in_new_terminal_buf now accepts a session name parameter and sets
  the buffer name directly via set_buf_name, eliminating the two-step
  intermediate-name approach (tool-N then set_buf_name rename)
- In restore_session, delete the ghost buffer unconditionally before the
  ghost_win check so its session name is freed before open_session or a
  subsequent toggle() tries to claim it, avoiding the E95 name-already-
  in-use error that broke both auto-restore and toggle reconnect
- Replace the nested tabpage/window loop used to find ghost_win with a
  single vim.fn.win_findbuf() call that searches all tabs in one shot
- Remove dead else branch from restore_session (ghost buffer deletion
  is now unified above the if ghost_win check)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add tool calling support via suspending SDK handlers

Tool calling bridge:
- ToolCallBridge converts Anthropic tool defs → SDK custom tools
- Handlers suspend (return unresolved Promise) when model invokes a tool
- tool_use content blocks streamed back in Anthropic SSE format
- Next request with tool_result resolves the handler, SDK resumes

SessionManager updates:
- Tracks tool bridges and pending tool call state per session
- Sessions with pending tool calls stay alive (not cleaned up)
- Tool result continuation routing via hasPendingToolCalls flag

Messages handler:
- Detects tool_result blocks in incoming messages
- Routes to pending session for continuation
- Supports text + tool_use mixed responses
- Streaming and non-streaming tool flows

Tests: 62 passing (11 new for tool-call-bridge)

fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging

commit.message:
[kernel] B: Fix zombie loss on exit

In RunningProcess::exit_thread(), the zombie thread list was consumed
at line 272 via self.zombie.take() to build zombie_threads. However,
the interrupted-threads branch at line 297 called self.zombie.take()
again, which always returned None because self.zombie was already
consumed. This silently dropped all zombie threads, causing subsequent
join_thread() calls to fail with "thread not found".

Fix: pass Some(zombie_threads) instead of self.zombie.take() in the
interrupted-threads branch, matching the pattern used by exit() and
the other branches of exit_thread().

Add zombie_join_pressure regression test that spawns workers sleeping
simultaneously so the first to wake exits while siblings are still
interrupted, exercising the buggy branch. The test fails without the
fix and passes with it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Fix unstuck PR loop to advance draft PRs instead of incorrectly skipping them (#609)

* Initial plan

* Fix unstuck PR loop to use check_pr_merge_status like advance_issue_pr

The main PR loop in run_unstuck only called has_failing_checks_for_pr(),
which returns false for draft PRs with no checks, incorrectly logging

commit.message:
Fix merge ralph skipping PRs with actual conflicts (#603)

* Initial plan

* Fix merge conflict detection: treat null mergeable as conflicting

When GitHub returns `mergeable: null` (still computing merge status),
the merge ralph was defaulting to `true` (no conflicts), causing PRs
with actual conflicts to be incorrectly skipped with "no conflicts,
skipping". Changed to `.unwrap_or(false)` to conservatively treat
null/missing as conflicting, matching the existing behavior in
promote_pending_merge_issues.
